commit.message:
feat(auth): add zustand auth store

Summary:
- add a Zustand-backed auth store wired to Firebase auth state changes
- initialize the auth listener from App and cover mount cleanup in tests
- add auth store tests and install zustand in the frontend package

Rationale:
- centralize client-side auth state in the repo's intended store layer
- keep Firebase auth state in sync without caching ID tokens in app state
- lock the behavior with tests before integrating follow-up auth features

Tests:
- npm run test:run -- src/stores/__tests__/auth-store.test.ts
- npm run test:run
- npm run build
- npm run lint

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
feat(frontend): add Firebase client bootstrap

Summary:
- add the Firebase SDK and a shared frontend bootstrap module that exports
  the initialized app, auth instance, and Google provider
- define Vite env typings, update `.env.example`, and add Vitest auth mocks
  plus focused tests for the new setup

Rationale:
- establish the client-side Firebase Auth baseline required by SHA-18 while
  keeping builds green when env values are unset
- provide reusable mocks so upcoming auth flows can isolate Firebase Auth
  behavior in tests

Tests:
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run build
- cd Shappar/frontend && npm run test:run

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
Add starvation hints and negotiation retry for collision fairness

DeepSeek lost all 16 collisions in a session due to passive insistence
choices and an unretried HTTP 500. Fix with two mechanisms:

- Starvation hints in reaction/negotiation prompts when an agent loses
  ≥2 consecutive collisions, informing them so they can self-adjust
- generateWithRetry for Tier 2/3 API calls (max 2 retries, linear
  backoff, abort-aware, cancellation-safe)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(checkout): pass variant data through all checkout flows (#1022)

Variant data (variantId, variantAttributes) was silently dropped during
checkout, causing "cannot call jsonb_each_text on a non-object" for
products with variants. The bug existed in web checkout, the API route,
and the mobile storefront.

- Zod schema: accept both camelCase and snake_case variant_attributes
- API route: read both key conventions, default to {} instead of null
- Web checkout: forward variantId/variantAttributes from cart to order
- Web cart hook: block add-to-cart when has_variants && !variantId
- Mobile: add variant_attributes to CartItem, build from selections,
  forward through checkout and order service, redirect ProductCard
  quick-add to detail page for variant products

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

⚡ Bolt: Add sizes prop to Next.js Image fill to improve LCP (#1024)

* perf(web): add sizes prop to Next.js image fill component in builder config

When using `fill` on a Next.js Image component without specifying `sizes`,
Next.js defaults to `100vw`. This forces the browser to download the
largest image size possible, significantly harming Largest Contentful Paint (LCP)
and overall performance on smaller viewports.

This change adds an appropriate responsive `sizes` prop.

Co-authored-by: ogabasseyy <196925138+ogabasseyy@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add sizes prop to file-uploader Image preview (CodeRabbit)

Grid uses cols-2/sm:cols-3/md:cols-4 so sizes reflect 50vw/33vw/25vw.

commit.message:
Structured LLM output, four-tier collision negotiation, CollisionResolvedEvent

Reaction output is now structured JSON ({ speech, insistence }) instead of
free-form text, with fallback parsing for backward compatibility. Collision
resolution replaces binary insist/yield with a four-tier system: pre-declared
insistence comparison (zero API calls) → multi-round three-level negotiation
(max 3 rounds) → bystander voting → random tiebreak, guaranteeing convergence.

New CollisionResolvedEvent domain event carries full resolution metadata
(tier, negotiation rounds, votes with raw text), enabling the history
projector to render tier-aware and perspective-specific descriptions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: Codex <codex@openai.com>

refactor: 重大重构 - 纠正对 OpenAI Harness Engineering 的误读

基于 Codex 深度评估，发现我们严重误读了 OpenAI 原文：

核心误读：
- 把"零手写代码"误读成"零人工干预"
- 把"team of agents"误读成"固定七角色流水线"
- 把"repo-specific"误读成"通用框架"
- 过度外推 OpenAI 内部数据

OpenAI 原文真正说的：
- "Humans steer. Agents execute."
- "Humans always remain in the loop"
- 人类负责：目标、优先级、验收、架构决策
- Agent 负责：写代码、测试、PR、响应 review
- 强依赖特定仓库，不能泛化

主要变更：
- README 完全重写，回归原文精神
- 去除所有"零干预""全自动"误导性表述
- 移除固定七角色流水线设计
- 承认当前实现是"概念文档+演示脚本"

下一步：
需要重新实现真正的 harness engineering：
- 单主循环（不是多角色）
- 结构化知识系统
- 可执行环境
- 机械化约束
- Execution plan 一等公民
- 持续 garbage collection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
Co-Authored-By: OpenAI Codex <codex@openai.com>

repository.name: harness-skill-v2
repository.description: Harness Engineering v2 - 零干预智能体团队系统，实现从PRD到部署的全流程自动化

commit.message:
fix(mobile-storefront): use AsyncStorage v3 API names (getMany/removeMany) (#1023)

PR #1011 incorrectly used v2 names (multiGet/multiRemove) because
the local node_modules had a stale v2.2.0 install. The lockfile
resolves v3.0.2 which renamed:
- multiGet → getMany (returns Record<string, string|null>)
- multiRemove → removeMany

Also fix iteration: v3 getMany returns an object, not tuples.

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

commit.message:
fix(realtime): stabilize dashboard loading and api access

Summary:
- add API-side CORS handling and origin configuration so the web
  dashboard can fetch runtime data from a separate dev origin
- harden the shared realtime resource hook so websocket acknowledgements
  only activate after subscription and first-load fetch failures degrade
  visibly instead of leaving infinite skeletons
- add regression coverage for CORS and realtime acknowledgement behavior

Rationale:
- the dashboard was surfacing plain browser fetch failures because the
  API returned no CORS headers for cross-origin requests
- the shared hook also amplified visual instability by treating failed
  initial loads as perpetual startup and by refreshing on pre-subscribe
  websocket acknowledgements

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api build
- pnpm --filter @symphony/web test

- pnpm --filter @symphony/web build

commit.message:
chore(web): align fixtures after dependency update

Summary:
- remove the stale runtime issue fixture fields that no longer exist in
  the shared contracts after the backend cleanup
- carry the pnpm dependency refresh, including the turbo bump and the
  matching lockfile update
- keep the generated Next.js env reference in sync with the current
  build output

Rationale:
- pnpm up surfaced that the web test-support fixture was still pinned to
  the old runtime issue shape and would fail the build
- the dependency and generated-file updates belong with the fix because
  they are the exact state validated by the successful web build

Tests:

- pnpm --filter @symphony/web lint

refactor(runtime): split runtime helpers and sync docs

Summary:
- pass app-boundary runtime env through orchestrator workspace hooks so
  repo-owned lifecycle hooks execute under the live runtime contract
- split runtime wiring into focused helpers for DB-backed observer
  persistence, realtime diffing, Codex launch/protocol handling,
  Linear GraphQL tool execution, and repo snapshot capture
- update backend docs to reflect the current pnpm-based evaluation flow
  and the real TypeScript runtime status

Rationale:
- workspace hook execution was the last meaningful backend parity gap
  left in the current single-host TypeScript runtime
- the large runtime files were carrying multiple responsibilities,
  which made ownership and testing harder than necessary
- the docs had drifted behind the implementation and still described
  removed wrappers and old stub-runtime assumptions

commit.message:
fix(runtime): align refresh and observability contracts

Summary:
- make /api/v1/refresh queue and coalesce manual refresh requests
  instead of blocking on a synchronous poll cycle
- wire SYMPHONY_SOURCE_REPO through the app boundary so repo-owned
  workspace reuse and reset logic is active in production
- merge run metadata updates, ignore volatile snapshot fields in
  realtime invalidation, and trim unbacked runtime issue history fields
- add regression coverage for refresh behavior, env wiring, and both
  sqlite and file-backed journal metadata merging

Rationale:
- the refresh endpoint was over-promising queued behavior while still
  doing the expensive work inline, which made the API misleading
- repo-owned workspace parity and durable run metadata were partly in
  place but not actually active or safe in the live runtime
- the runtime issue contract should match the data we truly back, with
  history handled by the dedicated timeline endpoint

Tests:
- pnpm --filter @symphony/db build
- pnpm --filter @symphony/db test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/contracts build
- pnpm --filter @symphony/contracts test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core build
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/core lint
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api typecheck
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api test
- pnpm --filter @symphony/api lint

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

fix(home-manager): harden package manager and zsh config

Summary:
- add a dedicated Home Manager module for uv, npm, pnpm, and
  bun safety settings
- move the uv package into that module and import it from the
  Home Manager default config
- make the zsh secrets loader accept export-prefixed entries and
  fall back to a known oh-my-zsh path when ZSH is unset

Rationale:
- keep package manager policy in one place while matching pnpm's
  XDG config lookup on this machine
- avoid shell startup regressions caused by export-style secrets
  lines or direct sourcing of .zshrc

Tests:
- nix run nixpkgs#nixfmt-classic -- modules/home-manager/default.nix
  modules/home-manager/languages.nix
  modules/home-manager/package-managers.nix
  modules/home-manager/shells.nix
- nix build --dry-run 'path:/Users/ryan/nix-config#homeConfigurations/personalArm64.activationPackage'
- home-manager switch --flake 'path:/Users/ryan/nix-config#personalArm64'
- zsh -fc 'source /Users/ryan/.zshrc'

Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

  is linear

  issue lookup, comments, and state transitions

- replace the in-memory tracker placeholder with the admitted runtime
  tracker path
- keep tracker behavior aligned with the Elixir implementation so the
  TypeScript runtime preserves the same scope and mutation rules
- close one of the two runtime-critical parity gaps before wiring the
  real Codex execution path

- replace the JSON run journal with DB-backed runs, turns, events,
  issue timelines, runtime logs, and GitHub ingress dedupe
- start a single-flight autonomous poller and add health, runtime
  logs, and issue timeline API endpoints

Rationale:
- move historical observability into a durable store that the API can
  query directly
- keep live orchestration state in memory while making failures and
  agent activity inspectable after the fact
- establish the persistence and scheduler boundaries needed before
  wiring the real Linear tracker and Codex runtime
